codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failed scrutiny report, the rerun handoff/transcript skeleton, the backend-worker procedure, and commit `34fbba0c609e53664a7830e6984071d469f747c3`. The rerun does rewire the active controller/facade paths so `JournalEntryService`, `PayrollAccountingService`, and `SettlementService` no longer call `super` directly for the touched flows, but it stops short of the required hard-cut: the original journal/payroll/settlement implementations still remain inside `AccountingCoreEngineCore`, and `AccountingIdempotencyService` still republishes the old settlement/payment routes through `super.*`. That leaves duplicate monolith owners and a surviving indirect path back into `AccountingCoreEngineCore`, so the original blocking decomposition failure is not fully resolved.

issues.description: The ownership hard-cut is still incomplete because `AccountingCoreEngineCore` keeps full business-logic implementations for the same touched behaviors the feature was supposed to extract: `createStandardJournal` (317), `createJournalEntry` (548), `postPayrollRun` (1705), `recordPayrollPayment` (1747), `recordSupplierPayment` (2252), `autoSettleSupplier` (2447), `settleDealerInvoices` (2490), and `settleSupplierInvoices` (2775). The packet copied those bodies into the new direct services, but it did not retire the monolith versions, leaving duplicate live owners instead of one canonical owner per behavior.

The prohibited indirect path back into the monolith still exists in code: `AccountingIdempotencyService` continues to expose public `recordSupplierPayment`, `settleDealerInvoices`, `autoSettleDealer`, `settleSupplierInvoices`, and `autoSettleSupplier` methods that delegate via `super.*` to `AccountingCoreEngineCore` (122-140). Even though the main callers were rewired, these shadow super-backed routes were not hard-cut or retired, so the original 'no indirect delegation back into `AccountingCoreEngineCore`' requirement is still not fully satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The accounting module guidance is stale after this ownership-extraction packet: it still documents `AccountingCoreEngineCore` as the centralized owner of journal creation and payroll posting, which no longer matches the intended service split reviewers and future workers are supposed to reason about.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/accounting/AGENTS.md:29` says `AccountingCoreEngineCore` is the centralized journal/payroll owner, but the current wiring routes those flows through `JournalEntryService` and `PayrollAccountingService` via `AccountingService.java:177-183` and `203-252`.

summary: FAIL: this rerun removes the direct `super` usage from the new journal/payroll/settlement services, but it does not finish the ownership hard-cut because the old implementations still live in `AccountingCoreEngineCore` and remain re-exposed through `AccountingIdempotencyService` super wrappers.
